description: Dynamic bridge — 2026-05-12 session — Re-escalation shock cascade, Brent $105+, Nifty closed below 24,000

market_regime.label: RE_ESCALATION_SHOCK_CASCADE_BRENT_105_NIFTY_BREAKS_24000
market_regime.key_risk: Trump rejected Iran peace proposal over weekend; Brent +4-6% to $105+; Nifty -1.49% close at 23,815.85 below 24,000 floor; BankNifty broke 55,000 floor to 54,439.90; PM Modi austerity message compounds growth fears.
market_regime.note: May 11 session was a magnitude shock vs forecast: CHOPPY_WITH_NEGATIVE_BIAS (-0.2% expected) became -1.49% (7x amplification). Trump-Iran standoff + Modi austerity message + Brent +4% combined to break BankNifty 55,000 floor and Nifty 24,000 floor in single session. Sentiment now fragile. Oversold technicals (most large-caps deeply oversold) set up potential bounce attempt May 12 but capped by unresolved geopolitics. Defense (M012 active) and energy security (Coal India) re-engaging; OMC (MRPL -4.53%, RELIANCE -3.27%) and aviation (INDIGO -4.94%) under pressure.

key_drivers: US markets opened weak on May 11 after Trump rejected Iran response; S&P 500 / Nasdaq 100 futures down ~0.1% intraday — overnight could compound if Wall Street closes red.
Brent at $105+ continuing to weigh — no overnight resolution signal expected.

Oversold technicals (Nifty -1.49% off prior local highs, BankNifty broke 55K) typically produce bounce attempts at open, but in a re-escalation regime, gap-down opens often see early-session continuation before recovery.
BankNifty closed below 55,000 — first hour will test 54,000-54,200 support if continuation; reclaim of 54,800 needed for relief setup.
risks: UPSIDE: Overnight Iran-US back-channel signal could reverse open to gap-up. Watch Pakistan/Oman mediation news.
DOWNSIDE: Hormuz incident or fresh Israel-Iran kinetic escalation → gap-down up to -0.8%.

session_direction.key_assumption: Brent crude above $104, rupee at 95.51 new low, FII selling Rs 8,437 cr on May 11, US-Iran ceasefire on life support — selling pressure continues with oversold bounce attempts likely capped at 23,800-23,850.

ceasefire_binary_matrix.note: Trump REJECTED Iran proposal — peace_deal_advancing collapsed from 0.35 → 0.10. Escalation upgraded from 0.30 → 0.60 on IRGC frigate incident + Brent spike to $115 + Trump explicit rejection. Stalemate at 0.30 (Trump did pivot to "very positive discussions" by May 4 — diplomacy not fully dead).

stocks_to_watch: NIFTY 23,815.85: Closed below 24,000 floor for first time. Watch 23,700-23,800 support intraday; reclaim of 24,000 = relief signal.
BANKNIFTY 54,439.90: Broke 55,000 floor. Watch 54,000-54,200 support; needs reclaim of 54,800 for relief setup. Below 54,000 = capitulation risk.
COAL INDIA 464.45 (+1.76%): Decoupled UP on -1.49% Nifty day. Energy-security thesis confirmed. P037 (448-470 by May 15) and P043 (475-500) tracking favourably.
ACME SOLAR 290.25 (+2.63%): Renewable de-correlation working. Watch for follow-through on solar premium narrative.
COCHINSHIP 1,716.50 (-3.39%): Defense premium fade today despite re-escalation — lagged. Should reload tomorrow if Iran-US standoff persists. M012 active.
ASTRA MICRO 1,135.20 (-1.06%): Held relatively well — defense reload candidate.
MAZDOCK 2,565.80 (-3.44%): Defense laggard today; reload candidate for M012 trade.
INDIGO 4,299.40 (-4.94%): Aviation crushed by Brent $105+. Avoid until Brent < $100 OR oversold bounce.
MRPL 160.33 (-4.53%): OMC GRM relief thesis unwinding. P044 (162-172 by May 8) likely closed BELOW_RANGE.
RELIANCE 1,388.20 (-3.27%): Largest-cap drag — refining margins squeezed at high Brent, retail/Jio segments offer no near-term offset.
GODREJPROP 1,794.30 (-4.23%): Realty hit by inflation expectation channel via crude. Watch 1,750 support.
SWIGGY 263.70 (-5.99%): New 52-week area testing. P007 (≤260 in 7D, set Apr 1) — long resolved. Capitulation risk continues.
URBANCO 126.06 (-9.74%): Worst single-day drop in portfolio. Liquidity/sentiment shock.
SETFGOLD 127.07 (-0.87%): Closed at upper edge of P043 range (122-127). P043 resolves tomorrow (May 12).
SBISILVER 246.96 (+0.58%): Lone metal-ETF winner on safe-haven flow.
